name: Gallery
description: Horizontal scroll-snap strip of captioned figures. Pure CSS overflow + scroll-snap — keyboard and touch scrolling come from the browser, zero JS. The next slide peeks in as the scroll affordance.

useCases: Screenshot strips in case studies — via the optional `gallery` field in the projects frontmatter
Any horizontal series of captioned figures that should not stack vertically

antiPatterns.reason: Moving content without user intent fails WCAG 2.2.2 (pause/stop/hide), steals focus and frustrates on touch.
antiPatterns.alternative: This component: the user scrolls, nothing moves on its own.

antiPatterns.scenario: Putting essential content only inside the gallery
antiPatterns.reason: Off-screen slides are easy to miss; the gallery is for supporting visuals.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep key information in the prose; the gallery illustrates it.

a11y: Scroll region with role=region and an aria-label naming the set
The track is keyboard-focusable (tabindex=0) — arrow keys scroll, CSS snap aligns
List semantics: slides are <li> with Figure/figcaption inside
scroll-behavior: smooth sits behind the global prefers-reduced-motion reset
